planets.name: Which
planets.summary: Archive cluster around which: 316 posts; 4 quote-backed receipts pinned.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2026-06-28
artifacts.excerpt:
Some reasons why she might want an interior point on her possibility frontier:

- Better treated than by someone on the Pareto frontier, because his BATNA is worse, so you can capture your shapley value
- More robust to perturbations, which happen over the course of a relationship (and with age)
- being on the Pareto frontier often isn't transitive
- Noisy estimation of said frontier, particularly b/c of hedonic adaptation, risking ruin.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, might, good, seems.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2025-04-30
artifacts.excerpt: Very interesting podcast with Oliver Habryka wanting to create common knowledge about a sense in which Open Philanthropy is a dead player now. https://t.co/wk61hIoU2L
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, might, good, seems.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2026-03-07
artifacts.excerpt:
Will Iranian missiles/shaheds exhaust US stockpiles, or will the US be able to destroy Iranian launch capacity? 

I built a simulator to find out and add some clarity to forecaster discussions, which were pretty split. In Monte Carlo simulations using reasonable ranges, Iran wins about 10% of the time.
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, might, good, seems.

artifacts.title: Which receipt from 2022-11-08
artifacts.excerpt: One lens through which one can look at current FTX troubles is as a "Satan's apple" scenario. This isn't a perfect analogue, but there are situations where playing for longer has a high expected value, but if you play forever, you predictably lose everything. https://t.co/vXTkzIKtqL
artifacts.proof_of_search: Community Archive row → normalized post → topic planet. Terms: which, might, good, seems.
